observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: 1) Direct the Inventory Committee to physically count all the Local Road Network and render a corresponding report thereon; and 2) Consequently, reconciliation be made between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the GSO based on the physical count.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor cause the constitution of an Internal Audit Service Office which shall review the efficiency and effectiveness of internal control system of the Municipality from time to time, and review the legality and completeness of the supporting documents for each transaction.

Hence, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality adopt the National Payroll System for efficient and accurate computation of salaries and wages and timely remittances of employees’ social security and health insurance accounts.

Thus, we recommended that Management instruct the GSO to work on the immediate disposal of the unserviceable PPE assets in order to abate the danger it pose while the same remained in the premises of the Municipality.

2.2 We recommended that Management:

a) Initially create an Inventory Committee (IC), comprising of
personnel recommended by the Circular who shall be temporarily
relieved from all their regular duties, to complete the physical
inventory of all PPEs in three (3) months or less;

b) The IC shall then prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan (PIP)to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10)
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking
activities;

c) The IC shall undertake the physical count according to the
approved PIP and the guidelines and procedures provided in the
Circular and submit a report thereon.

3.4 We recommended that Management mandate the BAC Chairman to
facilitate the submission to GPPB of the approved APPs, PMRs and
APCPI results through the electronic mail address below within the
deadline set by GPPB as follows:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 4) Instruct both the GSO and Municipal Accountant to exercise the inventory control process as enumerated in MNGAS for LGUs. 5) Henceforth, record the receipt of supplies as inventory instead of directly recording such as expense.

1.7 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Cause the immediate constitution of an Inventory Committee to
conduct the extensive physical count of road networks and
prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the
General Services Officer (GSO) with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.
c) Require the GSO to maintain a local road inventory and road map,
and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components; and
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the local roads
network accounts based on the result of the physical count and maintain
necessary ledgers and records thereon.

2.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor cause the constitution of
an Internal Audit Service Office which shall review the efficiency and
effectiveness of internal control system of the Municipality from time
to time, and review the legality and completeness of the supporting
documents for each transaction.

3.8 Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct both the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant to exercise the inventory control process as
enumerated in MNGAS for LGUs.
b) Henceforth, record the receipt of supplies as inventory instead
of directly recording such as expense.

4.6 Hence, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the
Municipality adopt the National Payroll System for efficient and
accurate computation of salaries and wages and timely remittances of
employees’ social security and health insurance accounts.
As such, this uncollectible account shall be subject of write-off.

4) Management require the Municipal Accountant to recognize any terminal leave benefits due to its employees in their book of accounts for proper recording and monitoring in pursuance to COA Circular 2015-009 and, in the absence of budget, disclosed it in the Notes to Financial Statements.

5) The Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and other employees concern to secure from the Local School Board a copy of Special Education Fund (SEF) Budget and faithfully adhere with the provisions of Sec. 100 of RA 7160 and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 on the implementation and utilization of the fund, thereafter furnishing the Office of the Auditor its Utilization Report and other mandatory reports corresponding thereto.

6) The Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to spearhead the establishment of its database as their basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which then will form part of their overall management information system.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare and submit the Summary of Real Property Tax Receivable to the Municipal Accounting Office at the start of the year or during the month of January; and
2. The Municipal Accounting Office to immediately set up the RPT/SET Receivables for the current year by preparing the corresponding JEV upon receipt of the Summary of RPT Receivable, and make the journal accounting entries for setting up of RPT Receivables, Deferred RPT Income, actual collection minus the discount and distribution to other LGU’s share.

1.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the
municipality to conduct the inventory on the physical count of its
road network and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the cost of local
roads and its components in their Books of Accounts; and
c) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and
the General Services Office with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects.

2.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to recognize any terminal leave benefits due to its employees in their
book of accounts for proper recording and monitoring in pursuance to
COA Circular 2015-009 and, in the absence of budget, disclosed it in the
Notes to Financial Statements.

3.7 We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and other employees concerned to secure from the Local
School Board a copy of Special Education Fund (SEF) Budget and
faithfully adhere with the provisions of Sec. 100 of RA 7160 and DEPED-
DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 on the implementation and
utilization of the fund. Thereafter furnish the Office of the Auditor its
Utilization Report and other mandatory reports corresponding thereto.

4.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal
Person to spearhead the establishment of its database as their basis for
gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation
which then will form part of their overall management information
system.

3. We recommended that the LGU adopt the National Payroll System for efficient and accurate computation of salaries and wages and timely remittances of employees’ social security and health insurance accounts.

4. We recommended that Management adopt the Negotiated Procurement (Agency-to-Agency method) for the procurement of office supplies. The LGU can obtained lowest price quotation from DBM-PS because all the items in their stock was previously bidden out from various suppliers.
5. We recommended that the LGU’s HOPE and BAC adhere to and strictly comply with the submission of Project Monitoring Report as required under 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

1.Conduct physical Inventory count of agency’s total PPE at least once a year by the Inventory Committee and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; 2.Maintain and update Subsidiary Ledger and Property Card for each item; 3.Put Property Identification Card/Property Tag to each item; and 4.Prepare Acknowledgment . Receipt for Equipment (ARE) each time one is issued to an official or employee.

We recommended that Management allocate one percent (1%) in the Annual Budget for the implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children as mandated under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-120.
observations.managementAction: Management did not appropriate in the Annual Budget

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management purchase directly from PS-DBM
observations.managementAction: There is no PS-DBM Depo in their locality.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare and
submit the Summary of Real Property Tax Receivable to the
Municipal Accounting Office at the start of the year or during the month of January; and the Municipal Accounting Office to
immediately set up the RPT/SET Receivables for the current year by
preparing the corresponding JEV upon receipt of the Summary of
RPT Receivable, and make the journal accounting entries for setting
up of RPT Receivables, Deferred RPT Income, actual collection minus
the discount and distribution to other LGU’s share.

2.5 We recommended that the LGU adopt the National Payroll System
for efficient and accurate computation of salaries and wages and
timely remittances of employees’ social security and health insurance
accounts.

3.4 We recommended that Management adopt the Negotiated
Procurement (Agency-to-Agency method) for the procurement of
office supplies. The LGU can obtained lowest price quotation from
DBM-PS because all the items in their stock was previously bidded
out from various suppliers.

4.4 We recommended that the LGU’s HOPE and BAC adhere to and
strictly comply with the submission of Project Monitoring Report as
required under 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended to management that submission of PO’s and other infrastructure contracts must be made within the prescribed period for prompt auditorial and technical review to avoid future suspensions in audit especially when there was already payment made on the contract.

We recommended that the management initiate creation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) as required under Section 12.b of R.A. 10121 otherwise known as Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010.
We recommended to management that LGU GFPS shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code.
Ensure that the LGU GFPS shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code.
Ensure that a bank reconciliation is prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant for the Trust Fund account.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value to determine the quantity of accountable forms received, issued and cancelled.

We recommended that Management initiate creation of LDRRMO under the Office of the Mayor. It shall be initially composed of a local DRRM officer to be assisted by three (3) staff responsible for 1.) administration and training, 2.) research and planning and 3.) operations and warnings.
We recommended that Management purchase directly from PS-DBM all the supplies needed as stated in the Annual Procurement of the agency.

We recommended that Management submit  the  PO’s and other infrastructure contracts within the prescribed period for prompt auditorial and technical review to avoid future suspensions in audit especially when there was already payment made on the contract.
Ensure that the LGU’s GAD Focal Point System shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code.

We recommended  that Management allocate one percent (1%) in the Annual Budget for the implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children in compliance with  DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
We recommend that Local Chief Executive should strictly adhere to the general policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund.
observations.reason: Some projects/programs and activities does not  comply with DBM-DILG JOINT CIRCULAR.

observations.recommendation: Direct the LDRRMO to prepare Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the transfer of the subject PPE accounts from the Trust Fund books to the General Fund, take up the unrecognized Income from Donations and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses. Prepare the related Journal Entry Voucher and submit the same to the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables
We recommend that Local Chief Executive should strictly adhere to the general policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development Fund.
Direct the LDRRMO to  prepare a Report on the Utilization of the DRRMF and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
Ensure that the LGU GFPS shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code.
Ensure that a bank reconciliation statement is prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor for the Trust Fund account.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt  of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund  transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare  the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms With and Without Money Value to determine the quantity of accountable forms received, issued and cancelled.
